title: [Bug]: --mount=type=cache RUN caches live in the VM on macOS
body:
### Issue Description

I am building an image, which uses a `RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/opt/software ...` command. I am also using macOS, where podman runs in a VM. I find that the cache lives inside this VM and not on the host.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Use podman on macOS
2. Build an image using a `--mount=type=cache`
3. Look into `/var/tmp` on the host -- no cache is found ther
4. Look into `/var/tmp` inside the VM that podman uses (`podman machine ssh`; `ls /var/tmp`): the cache is there


### Describe the results you received

The cache lives inside the VM

### Describe the results you expected

The cache lives on the host

### podman info output

```shell
$ podman --version
podman version 4.3.1
$ uname -a
Darwin redshift.local 22.2.0 Darwin Kernel Version 22.2.0: Fri Nov 11 02:08:47 PST 2022; root:xnu-8792.61.2~4/RELEASE_X86_64 x86_64 i386 Darwin
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

I installed podman via MacPorts

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: Hi @eschnett , on `macOS` all the builds are executed inside the VM so cache stays there so other builds can reuse it, otherwise it defeats the purpose of `--mount=type=cache`.
Would it be possible to mount `/var/tmp/buildah-cache` from the host, i.e. the macOS system? This would still work as a cache, and would make managing the cache more similar to what happens on Linux.
You should be able to do this by yourself via the podman machine command. Or edit the containers.conf, I believe.

The home directory seems to be mounted via this stanza:
```JSON
  {
   "ReadOnly": false,
   "Source": "/Users/eschnett",
   "Tag": "vol0",
   "Target": "/Users/eschnett",
   "Type": "9p"
  }
```

What should I use for `Tag` and `Type`, respectively, for `/var/tmp/buildah-cache`? Do you have a pointer to documentation?

[machine]
volumes = [
   "$HOME:$HOME",
   "/var/tmp/buildah-cache:/var/tmp/buildah-cache".
]

In containers.conf might work.  You might need to recreate the machine.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since you have a solution, I can close.
